items.title: AI researcher claims he's already bypassed Anthropic's Fable 5 guardrails
items.summary: “Pliny the Liberator,” says he has been “cleverly finding the holes in the fence that the thought police missed,” in the newly launched Fable 5.

items.title: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.summary: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.

items.title: Kalshi now requires users to reveal employers as it fights insider trading and market manipulation
items.summary: The prediction markets platform introduced new measures on Tuesday that it said will apply to markets it considers are likely to face higher risks of insider trading and abuse.

items.title: Anchorage backs Treasury’s GENIUS AML rules, seeks secondary-market sanctions clarity
items.summary: A public comment letter argues that regulated stablecoin issuers need clearer compliance standards to avoid sanctions risks tied to secondary-market activity.

items.title: AI deepfake election ad in Minnesota raises transparency concerns
items.summary: A deepfake campaign attack ad in the US state of Minnesota has raised questions around the ethics of using AI in political ads and the regulations surround AI use.

items.title: Singapore bank DBS to offer tokenized gold to retail customers
items.summary: Each token is backed by one gram of physical gold held by DBS in a dedicated vault in Singapore.

items.title: Live updates: Softer core inflation gave crypto a bounce, but only bitcoin held up on the week
items.summary: May CPI ran hot on energy and cooler underneath, lifting majors on Thursday, though ether and the large alts are still down 6% to 8% over seven days.

items.title: It's not just bitcoin ETFs. Corporate BTC buying has dried up too
items.summary: ETF outflows have dominated the narrative but corporate bitcoin treasuries have gone quiet too, compounding the demand-side weakness.

items.title: XRP holds above $1.10 as ETF inflows rise, but traders remain cautious
items.summary: XRP bounced from recent lows on a late volume surge, though the token continued to lag the broader crypto market as futures traders positioned around key support levels.

items.title: Bitcoin has reached a deep bear-market valuation zone. The hard part may come next.
items.summary: Two widely watched gauges show capitulation, but the analyst flagging them warns the slow grind comes next.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: TradFi advisers want stablecoins, tokenization over Bitcoin: Bitwise
items.summary: Bitwise’s Matt Hougan says it was “pretty hard to engage with advisers on Bitcoin” during recent discussions, who are more interested in stablecoins and tokenization.

items.title: Teen crypto scammer stole $13M to splurge on private jets, Lambo
items.summary: Trenton Richard Johnston was arrested in March during a traffic stop for speeding, but investigators soon found he was involved in a wider fraud scheme.

items.title: Analysts tip pressure for Bitcoin, gold as US inflation tops 4%
items.summary: “We continue to view the current macro environment as a headwind for Bitcoin,” 10x Research’s Markus Thielen said.

items.title: Delaware, New Jersey advance bills banning crypto ATMs
items.summary: Lawmakers in Delaware and New Jersey have advanced laws that would completely ban crypto ATMs, a measure that has only been enacted in three US states.

items.title: XRP transaction demand falls 91.5% as traders focus on $0.65 support
items.summary: XRP network activity and investor profitability fall to record lows, leading traders to focus on the $1 to $0.65 zone.

items.title: Privacy returns to focus as Ethereum developers explore new token standards
items.summary: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at where privacy is headed in the Ethereum ecosystem.

items.title: BlackRock and Fidelity are quietly turning bitcoin ETFs into a two-firm market
items.summary: BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC are attracting the vast majority of new bitcoin ETF money, leaving smaller funds increasingly sidelined as institutional investors consolidate around the industry's largest players.

items.title: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
items.summary: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: Who answers the 3am call when DeFi breaks?
items.summary: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Ben Nadareski writes that to win over big investors, DeFi builders must act like accountable money managers, not just software developers. Then, Stephen Stonberg says that bitcoin holders can survive crashes and protect their assets by earning
